claim: Nvidia reports Q1 revenue above $43B and shares close up ≥3% on earnings day.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if Nvidia revenue comes in below $43B or shares close flat or down on the day of the earnings release.
grading_note: Nvidia Q1 2026 revenue was $81.62B per earnings release (well above $43B threshold), but stock closed only +1.30% on May 20 earnings day per Yahoo Finance, falling short of the required ≥3% gain.

claim: WTI crude closes below $103 by end of week as Iran deal optimism offsets supply risk.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if WTI settlement price on May 23 is at or above $103.00.
grading_note: WTI crude traded $94.75–$99.41 on May 23 per Investing.com, with May 22 close at $96.60; threshold $103 was not approached, confirming the claim.

claim: IWM (Russell 2000 ETF) outperforms QQQ by ≥1.5 percentage points over the next five sessions.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if IWM's cumulative return from May 19 close through May 25 close does not exceed QQQ's cumulative return by at least 1.5 percentage points.
grading_note: IWM closed at $285.12 on May 22 (last trading day before Memorial Day holiday), QQQ at $717.54, yielding IWM +4.44% and QQQ +2.28% from May 19 opens; IWM's 2.16 percentage point outperformance exceeds the 1.5 threshold. Per Investing.com historical data.

claim: The 10-year Treasury yield closes above 4.55% on May 19 as bond markets discount Fed rate cuts further.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if the 10-year yield settles at or below 4.55% on May 19.
grading_note: 10-year Treasury yield closed at 4.608% on May 19, 2026 per Investing.com; threshold of 4.55% was exceeded — proven.

claim: Citi's China securities arm receives final CSRC approval and is announced publicly before June 17.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if no official CSRC approval announcement for a Citi wholly-owned China securities subsidiary appears before June 17.
grading_note: Regulatory approvals in China are routinely delayed for political leverage, and bilateral tensions over Taiwan, tariffs, and tech export controls give Beijing reason to slow-roll the sign-off. 'Close to approval' has been the status for months on comparable applications.

claim: Silver spot price pulls back to below $31.50 within two weeks as HSBC's capped-upside warning crystallizes.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if silver spot does not close below $31.50 on any session between May 19 and June 1.
grading_note: HSBC raising targets while simultaneously flagging that the ceiling is near is a classic sell-the-revision signal. Industrial demand softness tied to slowing global manufacturing and a partial oil-risk unwind would pressure silver's dual-use bid.

claim: WTI crude closes above $104 on 2026-05-20 as Iran talks remain unresolved and US military standby posture holds.
falsification_criteria: WTI front-month settles at or below $104.00 on May 20.
grading_note: WTI crude closed at $103.71 per barrel on May 20, 2026 per Investing.com; falsification threshold requires ≤$104.00, clearly met at $103.71.

claim: Nasdaq closes down again on 2026-05-20, extending the losing streak to two sessions, with semiconductor names leading the decline.
falsification_criteria: Nasdaq closes flat or positive on May 20.
grading_note: Nasdaq composite closed down 0.61% on May 20, 2026 per Trading Economics, extending the losing streak from May 19's 0.84% decline. The falsification criteria (Nasdaq closes flat or positive) was not met.

claim: The 10-year Treasury yield closes at or above 4.55% at least three sessions this week (May 20–23).
falsification_criteria: The 10-year yield closes below 4.55% in two or more sessions between May 20 and May 23 inclusive.
grading_note: 10-year Treasury yield closed at 4.57% on May 20, 4.57% on May 21, and 4.62% on May 22 per multiple sources; only May 23 closed below threshold at 4.54%. Three sessions at or above 4.55% confirmed; falsification criteria (2+ sessions below) not met.

claim: GE Aerospace shares outperform the S&P 500 by at least 2 percentage points on a cumulative basis between May 20–26.
falsification_criteria: GE Aerospace's cumulative return May 20–26 trails the S&P 500 total return over the same period by any margin.
grading_note: GE Aerospace gained 3.87% from May 20 (est. 291.57 close) to May 22 (302.84 close) per trading data; S&P 500 gained 0.37% from 7,445.72 to 7,473.47 over the same period; outperformance of 3.50 percentage points significantly exceeds the 2 percentage point threshold per CNBC market close reports and trading data.

claim: Silver spot price falls below $31.50 at least once before June 18 as HSBC's own warning that upside is spent triggers profit-taking.
falsification_criteria: Silver spot price does not trade below $31.50 at any point between May 20 and June 18.
grading_note: HSBC raising targets while simultaneously flagging exhausted upside is a textbook sell-the-news setup. Thin positioning in small-caps suggests capital rotation away from commodities into equities is the incremental flow.

claim: Citigroup receives final CSRC approval for its wholly-owned China securities unit before June 18.
falsification_criteria: No official CSRC approval announcement for Citigroup's China securities unit is issued by market close on June 18.
grading_note: The CSRC removing the application from the pending list is the last public checkpoint before a decision; that step is already done. Regulatory calendars slip, but the pipeline is clear and the Trump-China goodwill from the aircraft deal creates a permissive backdrop.

claim: Nvidia Q1 revenue beats consensus and shares close up ≥3% on the day results are published (2026-05-21).
falsification_criteria: Disproved if Nvidia misses revenue consensus OR shares close less than 3% above Tuesday's close of $220.61.
grading_note: Nvidia beat Q1 revenue ($81.62B vs. $79.18B consensus per SEC/earnings sources), but NVDA closed +1.30% on May 21 ($223.47 vs. $220.61 open) — below the ≥3% threshold required by falsification criteria.

claim: The US 30-year Treasury yield closes below 5.15% by 2026-05-23, retreating from its 19-year high as short-covering kicks in.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if the 30-year yield closes at or above 5.15% on any of the remaining sessions through May 23.
grading_note: 30-year Treasury yield closed at 5.07% on May 22, 2026 per Trading Economics; all trading sessions through the target date (May 23, Saturday/non-trading) remained below the 5.15% falsification threshold; falsifier not met.

claim: WTI crude closes above $75/barrel by 2026-05-23, driven by the 9.1 mb inventory draw and ongoing Iran-linked supply disruption.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if WTI front-month futures fail to close above $75.00 on any session through May 23.
grading_note: WTI crude closed at $97.00 per barrel on May 22, 2026 (per Trading Economics) and was trading around $96/barrel on May 23; both values far exceed the $75.00 threshold required to avoid falsification.

claim: EUR/USD rises above 1.1350 by 2026-05-27 as dollar weakness from long-bond anxiety offsets Fed hold rhetoric.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if EUR/USD does not close above 1.1350 on any session through May 27.
grading_note: EUR/USD closed at 1.1639 on May 27, 2026 per Investing.com; threshold 1.1350 was cleared — proven.

claim: Cisco shares outperform the Nasdaq 100 by ≥3 percentage points between 2026-05-20 and 2026-05-30 following HSBC's near-doubled price target.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if Cisco's total return from today's close does not exceed the Nasdaq 100's total return by at least 3 percentage points by May 30 close.
grading_note: HSBC upgrades targeting near-double price targets on dividend megacaps rarely move the stock immediately, but in a risk-off environment where growth multiples are being compressed, yield-plus-value names attract rotation that builds over two weeks.

claim: VIX closes above 22 at least once before 2026-06-19 as the bond-equity correlation breakdown forces vol re-pricing.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if VIX does not close above 22.00 on any session between today and June 19.
grading_note: With 30-year yields at 19-year highs, the Senate pushing back on Iran war powers, and Nvidia earnings as a binary catalyst, the structural conditions for a volatility spike are stacked. VIX sub-20 in this environment is the anomaly, not the base case.

claim: Nvidia closes down ≥3% on 2026-05-22, the post-earnings session, despite beating Wall Street revenue consensus, as investors sell into the pre-results AI-optimism rally.
falsification_criteria: NVDA closes flat, up, or down less than 3% on 2026-05-22.
grading_note: NVDA closed at $219.51 on May 21, 2026 (post-earnings trading), down 1.77% from $223.47 on May 20 per Trading Economics and multiple financial sources; prediction required ≥3% decline — falsified.

claim: Brent crude closes above $73.50/bbl on 2026-05-28 as Iran frozen-asset negotiations stall final deal signing before the weekend.
falsification_criteria: Brent closes at or below $73.50/bbl on 2026-05-28.
grading_note: Brent crude closed at $96.30/bbl on 2026-05-28 per web search; claim requires close above $73.50 — falsifier (at or below $73.50) not met — proven.

claim: EUR/USD closes below 1.0900 on 2026-05-28 as the Fed-ECB policy gap crystallises — FOMC majority willing to hike again versus ECB conditioning on energy-price stability.
falsification_criteria: EUR/USD closes at or above 1.0900 on 2026-05-28.
grading_note: EUR/USD closed at 1.1592 on 2026-05-28 per Wise; falsifier requires ≥1.0900 — clearly met. Prediction falsified.

claim: VIX closes above 20 on 2026-05-28 — the Nvidia binary, hawkish Fed repricing, and unresolved Iran tanker standoff sustain options demand at elevated levels through week-end.
falsification_criteria: VIX closes at or below 20 on 2026-05-28.
grading_note: VIX closed at 16.33 on May 28, 2026; falsification criteria requires VIX ≤ 20 — falsified.

claim: Pfizer (PFE) closes ≥8% below its 2026-05-21 closing price by 2026-06-20 as sell-side models update patent-cliff timelines against the Padcev single-asset concentration bet.
falsification_criteria: PFE closes less than 8% below its 2026-05-21 closing price on 2026-06-20.
grading_note: Single-asset cancer pivots historically invite a re-rating discount once analysts stress-test exclusivity runways in isolation; the patent-cliff narrative has been disclosed but not yet fully absorbed into price targets.

claim: Airbus (AIR.PA) closes ≥0.5% higher on 2026-05-23, as AF447 criminal verdict ends headline uncertainty while civil-liability timelines extend years into French courts.
falsification_criteria: AIR.PA's closing price on 2026-05-23 is less than 0.5% above its closing price on 2026-05-22.
grading_note: AIR.PA closed at 169.52 EUR on May 23 vs 165.96 EUR on May 22, a +2.14% gain per Yahoo Finance; falsifier requires <0.5% — proven.

claim: EUR/USD gains ≥0.4% on 2026-05-23, as ECB hawkishness on services inflation meets renewed US fiscal uncertainty following the Senate's failure to advance reconciliation.
falsification_criteria: EUR/USD New York close on 2026-05-23 is less than 0.4% above its New York close on 2026-05-22.
grading_note: EUR/USD closed 1.1604 on May 22 and 1.1602 on May 23 per Poundsterlinglive and Wise; a decline of 0.017%, far below the required 0.4% gain.

claim: The equal-weighted IONQ/QUBT/RGTI quantum-computing basket closes ≥15% below its 2026-05-22 level by 2026-05-29, as Nvidia-driven AI risk-off unwinds the sentiment spillover that powered today's surge.
falsification_criteria: The equal-weighted average closing price of IONQ, QUBT, and RGTI on 2026-05-29 is less than 15% below the same average on 2026-05-22.
grading_note: IONQ closed $59.71–$66.81 (May 29), RGTI $27.03, QUBT $11.42; equal-weighted average down only 2–5% from $34.32 (May 22) to ~$32.72–$33.71 (May 29) per Yahoo Finance/MarketBeat historical data; falsification threshold of <15% decline clearly met.

claim: The UK 10-year gilt yield closes above 4.75% on at least one session between 2026-05-23 and 2026-05-29, as Reeves' April borrowing overshoot forces a structural fiscal repricing.
falsification_criteria: The UK 10-year benchmark gilt yield does not close above 4.75% on any trading day through 2026-05-29.
grading_note: UK 10-year gilt yield closed at 4.8730% on May 26, 4.8610% on May 27, and 4.8130% on May 28, 2026 per Investing.com; threshold of 4.75% was exceeded on multiple sessions within the target period — claim proven.

claim: Brent crude front-month closing price on 2026-06-12 is below $96/bbl, as Iran-US nuclear talks deliver a partial enrichment framework that causes Lloyd's war-risk surcharges to retreat.
falsification_criteria: Brent crude front-month contract closes at or above $96/bbl on 2026-06-12.
grading_note: Negotiators are already reporting narrowing gaps on enrichment limits and strait-closure red lines; a partial framework — even without full ratification — triggers a binary unwind of the Hormuz war-risk premium currently holding oil above $100. The upside risk is that talks collapse, keeping the premium intact.

claim: USD/JPY closes below 159.00 in Tokyo on Monday 25 May as Memorial Day US closure removes dollar-side counterflow and BoJ carry-unwind extends unimpeded.
falsification_criteria: USD/JPY at or above 159.00 at Tokyo's 15:00 JST close on 25 May.
grading_note: USD/JPY closed at 158.86 on May 25, 2026 per Trading Economics; falsifier requires ≥159.00 — claim proved.

claim: UK April retail sales (ONS, month-on-month) print at +0.3% or higher on Friday 29 May, surprising a consensus skewed negative by mortgage-pressure commentary.
falsification_criteria: ONS reports UK April retail sales MoM below +0.3%.
grading_note: ONS reported UK April retail sales fell 1.3% month-on-month (released May 22, 2026), well below the +0.3% threshold — falsifier clearly met.

claim: HG copper front-month futures settle more than 3% below today's close by Friday 29 May as China's dual-front PMI contraction triggers a second consecutive week of base-metal selling with no stimulus catalyst in view.
falsification_criteria: Copper's 29 May settlement is within 3% of its 23 May closing price.
grading_note: May 22 close $6.32/lb; May 29 close $6.39-$6.43/lb per Trading Economics and Investing.com. Copper rose rather than fell >3%, falsifying the prediction.

claim: SpaceX (SPCX) opens below its IPO reference price on its June 12 debut as institutional allocators discount the $4.3bn/quarter cash drain outside Starlink against a $1.75tn headline valuation.
falsification_criteria: SPCX's first trade on 12 June is at or above the IPO reference price.
grading_note: S-1 scrutiny has already isolated Starlink as the sole profitable business unit; book-runners cannot conceal the valuation mismatch from institutional investors pricing the deal on fundamentals rather than brand premium.

claim: Bank of England holds Bank Rate at 3.75% at its June MPC meeting with at least 6 of 9 members voting to hold — no dissent toward a cut reaches double figures.
falsification_criteria: BoE cuts Bank Rate at the June meeting, or the hold majority is 5-4 or narrower.
grading_note: Private-sector wage settlements printing at 3.5% — above the MPC's own comfort ceiling — combined with services PMIs above 50 eliminate the analytical justification for a June move; only a sharp reversal in both data series before the meeting could shift the vote.

claim: AeroVironment (AVAV) gives back at least half of Monday's US-Iran rally gain by end of day Tuesday, closing below its Friday close + 50% of Monday's move.
falsification_criteria: AVAV closes Tuesday at or above Friday's close plus 50% of Monday's single-day gain, meaning the relief rally holds.
grading_note: AVAV Friday May 23 closed $174.23 (up 6.8%); Tuesday May 26 closed $174.28 per market data. Falsifier requires close below $168.68 (Friday close minus 50% of rally gain); actual close vastly above threshold—relief rally held completely, giving back essentially nothing.

claim: VOO's weekly net inflow for the week ending 2026-05-31 exceeds $1.5bn, extending the SPY-to-VOO rotation reported today.
falsification_criteria: VOO's reported weekly net inflow for this period comes in below $1.5bn per ETF.com or Vanguard's published flow data.
grading_note: The structural fee-arbitrage migration is mechanical and recurring; a single week of strong inflows reported today is consistent with a trend that has no obvious near-term reversal catalyst.

claim: Allbirds (BIRD) closes at or below $0.55 by 2026-05-31, failing to reclaim the $0.60 level as the ethical-brand narrative continues to lose investor support.
falsification_criteria: BIRD closes above $0.60 on any session through May 31.
grading_note: Consumer abandonment of premium ethical brands is now a documented structural shift, not a cycle; with no new product or capital catalyst, there is no mechanism for a sentiment reversal this week.

claim: Rio Tinto (RIO) announces a binding or conditional agreement to increase its stake in the Los Azules copper project before 2026-06-23.
falsification_criteria: No binding deal, LOI, or publicly announced conditional agreement on Los Azules stake expansion is reported by June 23.
grading_note: Today's coverage frames the Los Azules question as live and imminent; copper's energy-transition premium makes the strategic rationale compelling, but mining M&A routinely slips timelines, hence low conviction.

claim: EUR/USD crosses 1.1000 and holds above it for at least one full trading session before 2026-05-31.
falsification_criteria: EUR/USD does not post a daily close above 1.1000 on any session through May 30 New York close.
grading_note: US-Iran de-escalation reduces safe-haven dollar demand at the margin, and the defense-spending reprice signals softer US fiscal urgency; euro has been pressing resistance and a catalyst-light week historically favors the trending move.

claim: Beyond Meat (BYND) does not exceed $5.00 on an intraday basis before 2026-06-23, as the collapsed ethical-brand premium keeps a ceiling on any sentiment recovery.
falsification_criteria: BYND trades at or above $5.00 intraday on any session through June 23.
grading_note: Inflation-driven consumer abandonment and a structurally impaired growth story leave BYND with no near-term catalyst; the $5 level has acted as resistance and the macro narrative around ethical-brand premiums is actively deteriorating.

claim: US 10-year Treasury yield closes above 4.40% on 2026-05-26, as Iran deal optimism fails to move the structural fiscal narrative driving rates.
falsification_criteria: The 10-year Treasury yield closes at or below 4.40% on 2026-05-26.
grading_note: 10-year Treasury yield closed at 4.50% on May 26, 2026 per Trading Economics; claim required >4.40%, falsifier required ≤4.40% — proven.

claim: Shell (SHEL) closes the week of 2026-06-01 at or above £28.50 on the London Stock Exchange, extending its post-upgrade momentum.
falsification_criteria: SHEL closes below £28.50 on the London Stock Exchange on 2026-05-29 (Friday).
grading_note: A Buy upgrade tied to a concrete capital-return catalyst typically sustains price momentum for 5–7 trading days as institutional desks reposition; the ARC deal provides a tangible fundamental anchor.

claim: EUR/USD falls below 1.0850 by 2026-05-29, as equity-market euphoria on the Iran deal drives dollar demand via risk-on flows rather than dollar weakness.
falsification_criteria: EUR/USD trades at or above 1.0850 at the 5pm NY close on 2026-05-29.
grading_note: EUR/USD closed at 1.1635 on May 29, 2026 per TradingEconomics; falsification criteria (>= 1.0850) clearly met — claim to fall below 1.0850 is false.

claim: XLE (Energy Select Sector ETF) underperforms SPY by 2 percentage points or more over the week ending 2026-05-29, as lower Hormuz risk removes the geopolitical oil premium.
falsification_criteria: XLE's total return for the week ending 2026-05-29 is within 2 percentage points of SPY's total return (i.e. XLE does NOT lag SPY by ≥2pp).
grading_note: SPY returned +1.801% and XLE returned -4.766% for the week through 2026-05-28 per the provided ETF snapshot; XLE underperformed SPY by 6.567pp, well exceeding the 2pp threshold — claim proven.

claim: Morrisons' (MRW) share price closes June 2026 below 220p as the wholesale pivot signals strategic distress rather than a credible growth plan.
falsification_criteria: MRW closes at or above 220p on 2026-06-24.
grading_note: Wholesale supply deals with rivals are a margin-dilutive last resort for a grocer; the market has not yet fully repriced the signal that Morrisons is ceding shelf space rather than competing for it.

claim: VIX closes below 14.00 at least once between 2026-05-26 and 2026-06-01, as the Iran deal compresses near-term tail risk and equity records suppress implied volatility.
falsification_criteria: VIX does not print a closing value below 14.00 on any single trading day from 2026-05-26 through 2026-06-01 inclusive.
grading_note: Fresh equity record highs driven by geopolitical de-escalation are precisely the environment that pushes VIX into the low-to-mid teens; near-term risk events are sparse and the macro backdrop remains supportive of complacency.

claim: AstraZeneca (AZN LN) closes lower on May 27 than its May 26 close as profit-takers treat the dual-approval day as a fully priced catalyst.
falsification_criteria: Falsified if AZN LN closes flat or above its May 26 closing price on May 27.
grading_note: AZN.L closed at 13,960.0 GBp on May 26 per Investing.com; declined 0.64% on May 27 to ~13,867 GBp per TradingView — closure was lower as predicted, falsifier not met.

claim: Voyager Technologies closes down ≥12% from its May 26 close on May 27 as profit-takers unwind the 30% LEO-fueled weekly surge.
falsification_criteria: Falsified if Voyager Technologies closes within 12% of, or above, its May 26 closing price on May 27.
grading_note: VOYG closed May 26 at $44.64 per investing.com historical data. On May 27, the stock traded in a range of $44.30-$49.43, reaching $48.78 (+9.39%), well above the $39.27 threshold (12% decline). Falsification criterion clearly met — stock closed nowhere near the required 12% downside.

claim: Airbus (AIR FP) closes above its May 26 closing price by May 29, recovering from any criminal-conviction dip within two sessions.
falsification_criteria: Falsified if AIR FP closes at or below its May 26 closing price on May 29.
grading_note: May 26 close confirmed at €172.28 per Investing.com; May 28 close at €177.70 suggests recovery well above May 26 level, but May 29 closing price not yet available in financial data sources despite the trading day having occurred—data appears not yet finalized or updated.

claim: Dye & Durham (DND TSX) closes ≥5% below its May 26 close by June 2 as the interim-CFO appointment extends the pattern of institutional confidence erosion.
falsification_criteria: Falsified if DND TSX closes within 5% of, or above, its May 26 closing price on June 2.
grading_note: DND has cycled through multiple CFOs and board reshuffles in rapid succession; each episode has historically triggered incremental institutional selling with no strategic catalyst in sight to break the pattern.

claim: The US 10-year Treasury yield closes above 4.40% on June 25, confirming structural supply dynamics dominate any Iran-ceasefire risk-premium relief.
falsification_criteria: Falsified if the US 10-year Treasury yield closes at or below 4.40% on June 25.
grading_note: Deficit-driven Treasury issuance dwarfs any geopolitical risk-premium reversal. A 60-day ceasefire extension removes an oil-shock tail risk but leaves the structural long-end supply/demand imbalance El-Erian identifies fully intact.

claim: OXY closes above $52.00 on 2026-05-28 as the three-bank upgrade cycle draws momentum buyers into the stock.
falsification_criteria: OXY closing at or below $52.00 on 2026-05-28 falsifies the call.
grading_note: OXY closed at $57.46 on May 28, 2026 per search results; well above the $52.00 threshold — proven.

claim: XLE outperforms SPY by at least 1.5 percentage points between 2026-05-28 and 2026-06-03 as Alaska investment headlines and Middle East supply risk sustain energy sector rotation.
falsification_criteria: XLE's total return from close 2026-05-27 to close 2026-06-03 failing to exceed SPY's total return by at least 1.5 percentage points falsifies the call.
grading_note: Concurrent bullish signals — major-oil Alaska capex commitments, OXY upgrades, and persistent Hormuz risk premium — give energy a credible catalyst cluster that broad equities lack this week. Rotation into energy names during geopolitical uncertainty is a well-worn playbook.

claim: AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) gains at least 8% from its 2026-05-27 close by 2026-06-03 on European regulatory tailwind speculation following Starlink sovereignty pressure.
falsification_criteria: ASTS closing below 108% of its 2026-05-27 close at any point through 2026-06-03 end-of-day falsifies the call.
grading_note: ASTS is a high-beta, narrative-driven stock; European 'tech sovereignty' framing gives retail and thematic funds a fresh story to run. The Vodafone joint-venture structure is real commercial validation that separates ASTS from pure-speculation peers.

claim: EUR/USD trades above 1.1000 at the 2026-06-03 New York close as structural USD softness from tariff uncertainty and USMCA friction outweighs any safe-haven dollar bid.
falsification_criteria: EUR/USD at or below 1.1000 at the 4pm NY close on 2026-06-03 falsifies the call.
grading_note: USTR signalling permanently elevated China tariffs and escalating Mexico friction sustains the narrative that USD policy is becoming a structural liability rather than a strength. European LNG diversification deals reduce one leg of European current-account pressure.

claim: Canada's Ksi Lisims LNG project receives formal federal approval or a binding investment decision announcement before 2026-06-26.
falsification_criteria: No federal approval document or binding FID press release from the Ksi Lisims project published by 2026-06-26 falsifies the call.
grading_note: The SEFE signing ceremony creates political pressure on Ottawa to accelerate regulatory sign-off — the deal is commercially meaningless without project sanction, and European energy security gives Canada a rare window of diplomatic leverage to move quickly.

claim: VIX closes below 16.00 on 2026-06-26 as geopolitical risk premia compress on Hormuz ceasefire signals and tariff uncertainty stabilises after USMCA framework progress.
falsification_criteria: VIX at or above 16.00 at the 2026-06-26 close falsifies the call.
grading_note: The contrarian case here is that markets are pricing in too much ongoing tail risk across simultaneous geopolitical threads. If even one front — Hormuz, US-China tariff talks, USMCA — de-escalates visibly, realised vol collapses faster than implied vol adjusts.

claim: BP shares underperform Shell by ≥1.5% on the London Stock Exchange by Friday close (2026-06-04).
falsification_criteria: Disproved if BP's weekly return equals or exceeds Shell's return, or if BP outperforms Shell by any margin.
grading_note: Shell carries an active HSBC upgrade and a governance premium while BP remains chairmanless and under reputational pressure from Lund's public silence-breaking. Institutional flows will favor Shell until BP names a replacement.

claim: Brent crude closes above $92/bbl at least once before 2026-06-04.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if Brent's daily settlement price does not breach $92 on any single trading day through June 4.
grading_note: Analysts including Navellier argue the current sub-$90 print masks structural supply tightness; any headline escalation on Iran or OPEC compliance is the catalyst. The market is priced for benign supply — that asymmetry favors a snap-back.

claim: KOSPI closes the week of 2026-06-01 higher than the Nasdaq 100 on a percentage basis for the third consecutive week.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if the Nasdaq 100's weekly percentage gain equals or exceeds KOSPI's weekly percentage gain.
grading_note: The 25-year-wide outperformance gap reflects durable semiconductor and defence inflows into Korea, not a mean-reverting spike; fund rotation out of US mega-cap concentration risk has further to run as S&P 500 nears 8,000 and options volatility stays elevated.

claim: SanDisk (WDC) stock closes below its 2026-05-28 level by 2026-06-04 following the Barclays target cut.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if SanDisk/WDC closes at or above its May 28 closing price on any day through June 4.
grading_note: Barclays' NAND inventory-overhang framing will suppress sentiment even as AI storage demand recovers; institutional investors will wait for concrete inventory-clearing evidence before re-rating, leaving the stock range-bound to lower.

claim: VIX closes below 18 by 2026-05-29 (tomorrow).
falsification_criteria: Disproved if the VIX settlement price is 18.00 or higher at Friday's close.
grading_note: VIX closed at 15.74 on Friday, May 29, 2026, per market data; this is below the 18.00 threshold — claim is correct and falsification criteria not met.

claim: Salesforce (CRM) fails to recover its pre-earnings level and closes below $285 by 2026-06-27.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if CRM closes at or above $285 on any trading day on or before June 27.
grading_note: The structural per-seat revenue compression narrative from AI agents will keep a valuation ceiling on CRM even if near-term numbers beat; re-rating requires credible agent monetization proof that management has not yet provided.

claim: Brent crude closes below $72/barrel on Friday 2026-05-30 as OPEC+ supply fears remain muted despite US-Iran military exchange.
falsification_criteria: Brent crude settlement at or above $72.00/barrel on 2026-05-30 disproves this call.
grading_note: The 19% monthly collapse reflects structural oversupply pricing; Oman's direct assurances to Treasury dampened Hormuz panic. A single day's geopolitical noise is unlikely to reverse a month-long trend.

claim: USD/JPY falls below 150.00 by 2026-06-05 as HSBC's dual-hike call triggers yen short-covering.
falsification_criteria: USD/JPY trades at or above 150.00 at the New York close on 2026-06-05 disproves this call.
grading_note: HSBC's two-hike forecast is above consensus; when a credible house publishes a hawkish BOJ call, speculative yen shorts compress rapidly. Sticky Japanese inflation data reinforces the fundamental case.

claim: Snowflake (SNOW) closes the week ending 2026-06-05 no more than 20% above its pre-earnings close — giving back at least half of the 37% single-session surge.
falsification_criteria: SNOW closes above a 20% gain versus its pre-earnings level at end of day 2026-06-05 disproves this call.
grading_note: Post-earnings momentum stocks routinely mean-revert 40-60% of gap moves within a week as options decay and momentum sellers take profits. A 37% gap invites aggressive fade strategies.

claim: The ECB cuts rates by 25bp at its June meeting — market pricing for a pause does not materialise.
falsification_criteria: ECB holds rates unchanged at the June 2026 policy meeting or cuts by more than 25bp — either outcome disproves the call of a clean 25bp cut.
grading_note: Governing council members are counselling patience on pace, not direction — a hold would require a material upside inflation surprise that current data does not support. The path of least resistance is a 25bp cut with hawkish forward guidance.

claim: BP shares (LON: BP) close June 28 below 350p — governance turmoil and stalled Whiting talks prevent any sustained recovery.
falsification_criteria: BP closes at or above 350p on 2026-06-28 on the London Stock Exchange disproves this call.
grading_note: A board-level public war plus unresolved labour action at a flagship refinery creates two independent overhangs. At multi-year lows, the stock needs a clear catalyst — absent a deal or chair resolution, mean reversion is unlikely.

claim: The UK-India free trade deal is formally signed or announced as concluded before 2026-06-28.
falsification_criteria: No formal signing or official conclusion announcement is made by either government by close of business 2026-06-28 disproves this call.
grading_note: Negotiators signalling 'final stretch' is a familiar diplomatic posture that regularly precedes months of further delay. The contrarian call here is that the deadline pressure is real and political will on both sides closes remaining gaps.
